question_text:
Number of Atoms

Given a string formula representing a chemical formula, return the count of each atom. The output should be a string of pairs (name, count) sorted by name, where count == 1 means no number after the name.

Constraints:

  • 1 <= formula.length <= 1000
  • formula consists of English letters, digits, (, and ).
  • formula is always valid.
input_format: A string formula representing a chemical formula.
output_format: A string of atom-count pairs sorted alphabetically by atom name, omitting count when it is 1.
constraints: formula is always a valid chemical formula
explanation:
Stack-Based Parsing

Use a stack of Counter dictionaries. On open paren, push new Counter. On close paren, pop, read following number, multiply all counts by it, merge into new top. On element (uppercase + lowercase letters), read following digits as count (default 1), add to top of stack. Merge stacks and format the output sorted.

Time: O(N²) worst case. Space: O(N).
answer:
from collections import Counter
def countOfAtoms(formula):
    i, n = 0, len(formula)
    def parse():
        nonlocal i
        count = Counter()
        while i < n and formula[i] != ')':
            if formula[i] == '(':
                i += 1
                inner = parse()
                i += 1  # skip ')'
                num = 0
                while i < n and formula[i].isdigit():
                    num = num * 10 + int(formula[i]); i += 1
                if num == 0: num = 1
                for k in inner: count[k] += inner[k] * num
            elif formula[i].isupper():
                j = i + 1
                while j < n and formula[j].islower(): j += 1
                elem = formula[i:j]; i = j
                num = 0
                while i < n and formula[i].isdigit():
                    num = num * 10 + int(formula[i]); i += 1
                count[elem] += num if num else 1
        return count
    c = parse()
    return ''.join(k + (str(c[k]) if c[k] > 1 else '') for k in sorted(c))
